task_name: FLS Training Simulation Compilation - Multiple Tasks
summary: This is an instructional/educational video montage demonstrating multiple FLS training scenarios and equipment setups for laparoscopic urologic surgery simulation. It includes demonstrations of: (1) endoscopic stone extraction with embedded stone in foam appendage, (2) suturing on Penrose drain with knot tying on bladder neck model, (3) intracorporeal suture placement on kidney replica, and (4) various FLS trainer setups with anatomical models.
reason: Video is instructional/educational content showing equipment setup and multiple task vignettes without a single continuous performance. Contains narration, labels, and educational overlays. Not a performance video of a single FLS task from start to completion.

demonstrated_techniques: Proper grasper manipulation with endoscopic instruments
Stone extraction technique using curved grasper
Penrose drain suture placement and fixation
Knot tying extracorporeally on bladder neck model
Intracorporeal suture placement on simulated kidney
Use of knot pusher for loop deployment
Proper handling of pre-tied ligating loops
verbal_cues: Practical Simulations to Prepare Trainees for Laparoscopic Urologic Surgeries
I failed several times and ruptured the inner balloon [trainee reflection on difficulty]
common_errors_addressed: Excessive force causing inner balloon rupture (shown with red balloon model)
Improper stone grasping technique (demonstrated with curved instrument)
Inadequate loop cinching (shown in loop deployment sequence)
teaching_content.video_purpose: Training preparation resource showing multiple FLS task setups and anatomical models used in laparoscopic urologic surgery simulation training

video_segments_identified.description: Curved grasper manipulating foam appendage with embedded stone
Purple and green tube models with blue suture, knot formation on can model
Yellow foam blocks with marked suture passage points
Bladder neck and ureter anatomical models with pre-positioned suture

confidence_rationale: Clear evidence of instructional content: labeled anatomical structures, narration visible in captions, multiple unrelated task setups, educational overlay text. This is definitively educational material, not a scored performance.
